title: Distephanus streptocladus
intro: Distephanus streptocladus, commonly known as the "African bitter leaf", is a plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family, native to tropical Africa. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, malaria, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antimalarial properties, offering potential health benefits. The bioactive constituents of D. streptocladus include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in the form of a tea or tincture.
taxonomy: Distephanus streptocladus is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to tropical Africa. The plant is a member of the tribe Cichorieae. Distephanus streptocladus is further classified as a member of the genus Distephanus.
